title: ventilago cinerascens
intro: Ventilago cinerascens, also known as the ash tree or white ash, is a tropical tree species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its white or gray bark and small white flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ventilago cinerascens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
